qualifying_org_definition: Public, quasi-public, or nonprofit organization (federal baseline; no Alaska-specific narrowing found)
verification_instrument: No state-specific form identified in accessible policy documents; DPA manual 602-1 L inaccessible during research; federal baseline self-reported statement from qualifying org applies per 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii). FSP 91 (Request for ABAWD Work Program Exemption) is the client-facing declaration form — it asks for the name of the community service site and supporting documentation showing hours per week, but is not itself the org verification letter.
hours_formula_detail: FSP 90 (ABAWD Pamphlet, rev 12/25) states volunteer community service must total 80 hours or more in a 30-day period, consistent with 7 CFR 273.24 federal baseline. No workfare formula used.
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Alaska DPA policy document (FSP 90, FSP 91, FSP 92, DPA manual 602-1 L, or FY26 E&T State Plan) restricts qualifying volunteer organizations to Alaska-based entities. The qualifying definition is simply 'public or nonprofit organization' — identical to the federal baseline at 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii). Absent any state-level restriction, federal default applies and an out-of-state 501(c)(3) such as Tended should qualify operationally.
geographic_waivers: Good Faith Exemption waiver approved by FNS covers all Alaska census areas and boroughs except the Municipality of Anchorage, effective November 1, 2025 through October 31, 2026. OBBBA provisions additionally authorize the Secretary to grant new Alaska good faith exemptions expiring no later than December 31, 2028, and to waive areas with unemployment rates 150% above the national rate. Alaska Native and American Indian individuals are also categorically exempt per FSP 90.
submission_deadline: FSP 91 instructs recipients to give the completed form and documentation to any DPA office when demonstrating compliance. DPA general change-reporting guidance requires reporting changes (including hours falling below 20/week) within 10 days of the change. No specific month-end deadline for proactive ABAWD hour submissions found; reporting is event-driven when hours fall below threshold, not a fixed monthly upload deadline.
notes: Alaska's ABAWD policy (DPA manual 602-1 L) confirms community service at a public or nonprofit org satisfies the 80-hour/month requirement; search result snippets show no evidence of a mandatory EDP, MOU, county assignment, or provider registration prestep, placing AK in Tier 1. Confidence is medium rather than high because the DPA web server was consistently unavailable during research, preventing direct reading of the full policy manual and leaving the specific verification instrument unknown. As of Nov 2025–Oct 2026, Alaska has a Good Faith Exemption waiver covering all areas except the Municipality of Anchorage, so ABAWD is largely unenforced statewide; abawd_enforced_2026 is set to 'partial' to reflect that Anchorage remains subject to enforcement. | REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-20): Adversarial second pass attempted to refute Tier 1 classification by searching for hidden EDP requirements, pre-approved volunteer site lists, mandatory work registration gating steps, and MOU requirements for nonprofit hosts. Successfully retrieved and read the full 97-page Alaska SNAP E&T State Plan FY26 (approved 09/25/2025, FNS-approved). Key findings: (1) Alaska's E&T program is 100% voluntary — all 16,699 work registrants are exempt from mandatory E&T participation; no EDP or E&T enrollment is required before volunteer hours count under 7 CFR 273.24. (2) Alaska does not operate a Workfare component — the plan lists Workfare as an option but it is not checked. (3) The E&T plan does not describe a community service component; the ABAWD general volunteer lane under 7 CFR 273.24(a)(2)(iii) is a separate compliance path from E&T. (4) No pre-approved volunteer site list found anywhere in the 97-page plan or any other accessible source. (5) MOU language in the plan (p. 56) applies exclusively to higher education institutions for vocational training cost parity under the E&T Vocational Training component — not to general nonprofit volunteer hosts. (6) The six E&T provider contracts (Catholic Social Services, Covenant House, Downtown Hope Center, Frontier Community Services, Goodwill, My House) are for E&T service delivery in Anchorage/Kenai/Mat-Su; a Tended nonprofit host need not appear on this list. (7) Work registration recorded in DPA EIS is an administrative certification step, not a gating requirement for ABAWD community service compliance. Zero evidence found of any hidden prestep blocking the general volunteer lane. Confidence elevated to 'high': the authoritative state policy document submitted to FNS has been directly read and confirms all Tier 1 clean criteria. Remaining unknowns: verification_instrument (DPA manual 602-1 L remains inaccessible; no state-specific form name found; federal baseline applies) and remote_stance (no Alaska policy document addresses remote volunteer work). | EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: FSP 90 (ABAWD Pamphlet, rev 12/25) directly confirms 80 hours in a 30-day period — federal_80 baseline, no workfare formula. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: no Alaska policy restricts qualifying orgs to in-state; FSP 91 and FSP 90 both say 'public or nonprofit organization' with no location requirement — marked 'silent', federal default permits out-of-state 501(c)(3). (3) Geographic waivers: Good Faith Exemption covers all AK except Municipality of Anchorage (Nov 2025–Oct 2026); OBBBA extends Secretary authority to grant new AK good faith exemptions through Dec 2028; AK Native/American Indian individuals categorically exempt. (4) Submission UX: FSP 91 instructs recipient to bring completed form + documentation to any DPA office (paper_county_office); DPA also accepts changes by fax (1-888-269-6520), email (hss.dpa.offices@alaska.gov), phone, and Alaska Connect online portal. Reporting cadence is effectively monthly (80-hour window is per 30-day period); no fixed monthly deadline found — reporting is triggered when hours drop below 20/week. (5) Form required fields: FSP 91 is the primary client-facing ABAWD compliance form; it requires recipient name, address, case number, name of the community service site, and supporting documentation showing hours per week. No org EIN, org signature, or notarization required on the form itself; org verification letter requirements are not explicitly codified in accessible sources.
